{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_3458eacb9c444d4f8cb84e5c","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage validate\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"fmt\"\n5\t\t\"strings\"\n6\t\t\"sync\"\n7\t)\n8\t\n9\t// ValidationFormatter provides a simplified, fluent API for formatting validation errors consistently.\n10\t//\n11\t// This builder wraps the ValidationError functionality with an ergonomic interface that allows\n12\t// you to construct validation errors step-by-step using method chaining.\n13\t//\n14\t// # Design Pattern\n15\t//\n16\t// ValidationFormatter uses the builder pattern to provide:\n17\t//   - Fluent, chainable methods for readable code\n18\t//   - Auto-generated suggestions when not manually specified\n19\t//   - Type-safe error construction\n20\t//   - Consistent error format across all validation types\n21\t//\n22\t// # Required Fields\n23\t//\n24\t// Only validationType is required (via NewValidationFormatter). All other fields are optional.\n25\t// If suggestions are not provided via WithSuggestions, they will be auto-generated based on\n26\t// the validation type and expected/actual values.\n27\t//\n28\t// # Common Usage Pattern\n29\t//\n30\t// The typical workflow is:\n31\t//  1. Create formatter with NewValidationFormatter(type)\n32\t//  2. Chain With* methods to set optional fields\n33\t//  3. Call Format() to generate the ValidationError\n34\t//\n35\t// # Example\n36\t//\n37\t//\t// Basic usage with auto-generated suggestions\n38\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n39\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n40\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n41\t//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n42\t//\t    Format()\n43\t//\n44\t//\t// With custom suggestions\n45\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_field\").\n46\t//\t    WithExpected(\"required\").\n47\t//\t    WithActual(\"\").\n48\t//\t    WithFieldName(\"email\").\n49\t//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Email is required\", \"Check user input\").\n50\t//\t    Format()\n51\t//\n52\t// # Extending for New Validation Types\n53\t//\n54\t// To add support for a new validation type:\n55\t//  1. Use NewValidationFormatter(\"your_type\") with a descriptive type name\n56\t//  2. Set appropriate fields using With* methods\n57\t//  3. Optionally provide custom suggestions via WithSuggestions\n58\t//  4. The formatter will automatically include the type in error output\n59\t//\n60\t// # Fields\n61\t//\n62\t// All fields are private to ensure the builder pattern is used correctly.\n63\t// Access them through the public With* methods and Format().\n64\ttype ValidationFormatter struct {\n65\t\tvalidationType string\n66\t\texpected       interface{}\n67\t\tactual         interface{}\n68\t\tcontext        string\n69\t\tresponseSnippet string\n70\t\tfieldName      string\n71\t\tpatternDetails string\n72\t\trangeInfo      string\n73\t\tvalidationDetails []string\n74\t\tcustomSuggestions []string\n75\t\tcategory       ErrorCategory\n76\t}\n77\t\n78\t// NewValidationFormatter creates a new ValidationFormatter for the given validation type.\n79\t//\n80\t// This is the entry point for the builder pattern. The validationType identifies what\n81\t// kind of validation was performed and is used to:\n82\t//   - Categorize the error in the error message\n83\t//   - Generate appropriate default suggestions\n84\t//   - Provide context for error handling\n85\t//\n86\t// # Parameters\n87\t//\n88\t//   - validationType: The category of validation (e.g., \"status_code\", \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n89\t//\n90\t//     Common validation types include:\n91\t//     - \"status_code\": HTTP status code validation\n92\t//     - \"error_message\": Error message pattern matching\n93\t//     - \"content_type\": Content-Type header validation\n94\t//     - \"status_code_range\": Status code range validation (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\")\n95\t//     - \"cors_headers\": CORS header validation\n96\t//     - \"response_structure\": Response body structure validation\n97\t//     - Custom types for domain-specific validation\n98\t//\n99\t// # Returns\n100\t//\n101\t// A pointer to a new ValidationFormatter instance ready for method chaining.\n102\t//\n103\t// # Example Usage\n104\t//\n105\t//\t// Basic status code validation\n106\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n107\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n108\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n109\t//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users\").\n110\t//\t    Format()\n111\t//\n112\t//\t// Content-Type validation with context\n113\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n114\t//\t    WithExpected(\"application/json\").\n115\t//\t    WithActual(\"text/html\").\n116\t//\t    WithContext(\"API response\").\n117\t//\t    Format()\n118\t//\n119\t//\t// Custom validation type with all options\n120\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_field_check\").\n121\t//\t    WithExpected(\"non_empty\").\n122\t//\t    WithActual(\"\").\n123\t//\t    WithFieldName(\"username\").\n124\t//\t    WithContext(\"User creation\").\n125\t//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Username cannot be empty\", \"Must be 3-20 characters\").\n126\t//\t    Format()\n127\tfunc NewValidationFormatter(validationType string) *ValidationFormatter {\n128\t\treturn &ValidationFormatter{\n129\t\t\tvalidationType: validationType,\n130\t\t}\n131\t}\n132\t\n133\t// WithExpected sets the expected value for validation.\n134\t//\n135\t// This method specifies what value was expected during the validation check.\n136\t// The expected value is compared against the actual value to provide clear\n137\t// error messages showing what went wrong.\n138\t//\n139\t// # Parameters\n140\t//\n141\t//   - expected: The expected value (can be any type: int, string, []int, etc.)\n142\t//\n143\t//     Common types:\n144\t//     - int: Single expected value (e.g., expected status code 200)\n145\t//     - []int: Multiple acceptable values (e.g., []int{200, 201, 204})\n146\t//     - string: Expected pattern or literal value (e.g., \"application/json\")\n147\t//\n148\t// # Returns\n149\t//\n150\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n151\t//\n152\t// # Example\n153\t//\n154\t//\t// Single expected value\n155\t//\tformatter.WithExpected(200)\n156\t//\n157\t//\t// Multiple acceptable values\n158\t//\tformatter.WithExpected([]int{200, 201, 204})\n159\t//\n160\t//\t// String pattern\n161\t//\tformatter.WithExpected(\"application/json\")\n162\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithExpected(expected interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n163\t\tvf.expected = expected\n164\t\treturn vf\n165\t}\n166\t\n167\t// WithActual sets the actual value received during validation.\n168\t//\n169\t// This method specifies what value was actually received during the validation check.\n170\t// The actual value is compared against the expected value to show the discrepancy\n171\t// that caused the validation failure.\n172\t//\n173\t// # Parameters\n174\t//\n175\t//   - actual: The actual value received (can be any type: int, string, etc.)\n176\t//\n177\t//     Common types:\n178\t//     - int: Actual numeric value (e.g., actual status code 404)\n179\t//     - string: Actual message or content received\n180\t//     - Other types as appropriate for the validation context\n181\t//\n182\t// # Returns\n183\t//\n184\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n185\t//\n186\t// # Example\n187\t//\n188\t//\t// Status code that was received\n189\t//\tformatter.WithActual(404)\n190\t//\n191\t//\t// Error message that was received\n192\t//\tformatter.WithActual(\"access denied\")\n193\t//\n194\t//\t// Content-Type that was received\n195\t//\tformatter.WithActual(\"text/html\")\n196\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithActual(actual interface{}) *ValidationFormatter {\n197\t\tvf.actual = actual\n198\t\treturn vf\n199\t}\n200\t\n201\t// WithContext sets additional context about the validation operation.\n202\t//\n203\t// Context provides valuable information about where or when the validation occurred,\n204\t// making errors easier to debug and understand. This is especially useful in logs\n205\t// and test output where multiple validations might be happening.\n206\t//\n207\t// # Parameters\n208\t//\n209\t//   - context: A descriptive string explaining the validation context\n210\t//\n211\t//     Common context examples:\n212\t//     - HTTP endpoints: \"GET /api/users/123\", \"POST /api/orders\"\n213\t//     - Operation descriptions: \"OAuth token validation\", \"User creation\"\n214\t//     - Test scenarios: \"Positive case test\", \"Negative case test\"\n215\t//     - Component names: \"Authentication service\", \"Database query\"\n216\t//\n217\t// # Returns\n218\t//\n219\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n220\t//\n221\t// # Example\n222\t//\n223\t//\t// HTTP endpoint context\n224\t//\tformatter.WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\")\n225\t//\n226\t//\t// Operation context\n227\t//\tformatter.WithContext(\"OAuth token validation\")\n228\t//\n229\t//\t// Test scenario context\n230\t//\tformatter.WithContext(\"Testing unauthorized access\")\n231\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithContext(context string) *ValidationFormatter {\n232\t\tvf.context = context\n233\t\treturn vf\n234\t}\n235\t\n236\t// WithResponseSnippet sets a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\n237\t//\n238\t// Response snippets provide crucial debugging information by showing the actual\n239\t// content that caused the validation failure. This is particularly useful when\n240\t// debugging complex response structures or unexpected error messages.\n241\t//\n242\t// # Parameters\n243\t//\n244\t//   - snippet: A truncated excerpt from the response (recommended: 100-200 characters)\n245\t//\n246\t//     Best practices:\n247\t//     - Keep snippets concise (100-200 characters recommended)\n248\t//     - Include relevant parts that show the problem\n249\t//     - Truncate long responses with \"...\" to keep errors readable\n250\t//     - Sanitize sensitive data before including in snippets\n251\t//\n252\t// # Returns\n253\t//\n254\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n255\t//\n256\t// # Example\n257\t//\n258\t//\t// Short JSON response snippet\n259\t//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"error\": \"invalid_token\", \"code\": \"AUTH_001\"}`)\n260\t//\n261\t//\t// Truncated HTML snippet\n262\t//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(`<html><body>Error: Page not found...</body></html>`)\n263\t//\n264\t//\t// Error message snippet\n265\t//\tformatter.WithResponseSnippet(\"Access denied: insufficient permissions for resource\")\n266\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) *ValidationFormatter {\n267\t\tvf.responseSnippet = snippet\n268\t\treturn vf\n269\t}\n270\t\n271\t// WithFieldName sets the specific field name where the error was found.\n272\t//\n273\t// This method identifies the exact field that failed validation, which is especially\n274\t// useful when validating structured data like JSON responses, form fields, or\n275\t// configuration objects.\n276\t//\n277\t// # Parameters\n278\t//\n279\t//   - fieldName: The name of the field where the error occurred\n280\t//\n281\t//     Common examples:\n282\t//     - Response body fields: \"error\", \"message\", \"detail\", \"code\"\n283\t//     - Form fields: \"email\", \"password\", \"username\"\n284\t//     - Nested fields: \"user.email\", \"data.items[0].id\"\n285\t//     - Headers: \"Content-Type\", \"Authorization\"\n286\t//\n287\t// # Returns\n288\t//\n289\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n290\t//\n291\t// # Example\n292\t//\n293\t//\t// Simple field name\n294\t//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"error\")\n295\t//\n296\t//\t// Nested field notation\n297\t//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"user.email\")\n298\t//\n299\t//\t// Array field notation\n300\t//\tformatter.WithFieldName(\"items[0].id\")\n301\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithFieldName(fieldName string) *ValidationFormatter {\n302\t\tvf.fieldName = fieldName\n303\t\treturn vf\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t// WithPatternDetails sets information about pattern matching failures.\n307\t//\n308\t// This method provides additional context when validation involves pattern matching,\n309\t// such as regex validation for error messages, format checking, or content verification.\n310\t// It helps explain what pattern was expected and how the actual value didn't match.\n311\t//\n312\t// # Parameters\n313\t//\n314\t//   - details: Information about the pattern that failed to match\n315\t//\n316\t//     Common examples:\n317\t//     - Regex patterns: \"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\"\n318\t//     - Format requirements: \"expected format 'user@domain.com'\"\n319\t//     - Pattern descriptions: \"must contain at least one uppercase letter\"\n320\t//     - Match details: \"pattern matched at position 5 but failed validation\"\n321\t//\n322\t// # When to Use\n323\t//\n324\t// Use this method when:\n325\t//   - Validating error messages against regex patterns\n326\t//   - Checking string formats (email, phone, UUID, etc.)\n327\t//   - Verifying content patterns in responses\n328\t//   - Any validation involving pattern matching\n329\t//\n330\t// # Returns\n331\t//\n332\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n333\t//\n334\t// # Example\n335\t//\n336\t//\t// Regex pattern explanation\n337\t//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"regex pattern 'invalid.*token' did not match\")\n338\t//\n339\t//\t// Format requirement\n340\t//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"expected format: YYYY-MM-DD\")\n341\t//\n342\t//\t// Content pattern description\n343\t//\tformatter.WithPatternDetails(\"must contain at least 8 characters\")\n344\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithPatternDetails(details string) *ValidationFormatter {\n345\t\tvf.patternDetails = details\n346\t\treturn vf\n347\t}\n348\t\n349\t// WithRangeInfo sets range boundaries for range validation failures.\n350\t//\n351\t// This method provides context when validating against ranges, such as HTTP status\n352\t// code ranges (4xx, 5xx), numeric ranges, or value intervals. It helps explain the\n353\t// expected boundaries and what range the actual value fell outside of.\n354\t//\n355\t// # Parameters\n356\t//\n357\t//   - info: Description of the range boundaries\n358\t//\n359\t//     Common examples:\n360\t//     - Status code ranges: \"400-499 (Client Error)\", \"200-299 (Success)\"\n361\t//     - Numeric ranges: \"0-100\", \"1-1000\"\n362\t//     - Descriptive ranges: \"positive numbers only\", \"non-negative integers\"\n363\t//\n364\t// # When to Use\n365\t//\n366\t// Use this method when:\n367\t//   - Validating HTTP status code ranges (e.g., expecting 4xx, got 2xx)\n368\t//   - Checking numeric value ranges (e.g., age 0-120)\n369\t//   - Verifying values within intervals (e.g., percentage 0-100)\n370\t//   - Any validation involving range constraints\n371\t//\n372\t// # Returns\n373\t//\n374\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n375\t//\n376\t// # Example\n377\t//\n378\t//\t// Status code range\n379\t//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"400-499 (Client Error)\")\n380\t//\n381\t//\t// Numeric range\n382\t//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"0-120\")\n383\t//\n384\t//\t// Descriptive range\n385\t//\tformatter.WithRangeInfo(\"positive integers only\")\n386\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithRangeInfo(info string) *ValidationFormatter {\n387\t\tvf.rangeInfo = info\n388\t\treturn vf\n389\t}\n390\t\n391\t// WithValidationDetails adds additional validation-specific details.\n392\t//\n393\t// This method allows you to provide granular, multi-line information about what was\n394\t// checked during validation and specifically what failed. This is useful for complex\n395\t// validations that check multiple conditions or have detailed requirements.\n396\t//\n397\t// # Parameters\n398\t//\n399\t//   - details: Variable number of detail strings to add to the error\n400\t//\n401\t//     Common uses:\n402\t//     - List specific conditions that failed\n403\t//     - Explain validation rules in detail\n404\t//     - Provide step-by-step validation results\n405\t//     - Document intermediate validation states\n406\t//\n407\t// # When to Use\n408\t//\n409\t// Use this method when:\n410\t//   - Validation involves multiple checks or conditions\n411\t//   - You need to explain complex validation rules\n412\t//   - Detailed debugging information would be helpful\n413\t//   - The validation logic is non-trivial\n414\t//\n415\t// # Returns\n416\t//\n417\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n418\t//\n419\t// # Example\n420\t//\n421\t//\t// Multiple specific failures\n422\t//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n423\t//\t    \"Password must be at least 8 characters\",\n424\t//\t    \"Password must contain uppercase letters\",\n425\t//\t    \"Password must contain special characters\",\n426\t//\t)\n427\t//\n428\t//\t// Detailed explanation\n429\t//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n430\t//\t    \"Expected 200-299 status range\",\n431\t//\t    \"Got 500 indicating server error\",\n432\t//\t    \"Server logs show database connection failure\",\n433\t//\t)\n434\t//\n435\t//\t// Step-by-step validation\n436\t//\tformatter.WithValidationDetails(\n437\t//\t    \"Step 1: Check format - PASSED\",\n438\t//\t    \"Step 2: Check length - FAILED\",\n439\t//\t    \"Step 3: Check content - SKIPPED\",\n440\t//\t)\n441\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithValidationDetails(details ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n442\t\tvf.validationDetails = append(vf.validationDetails, details...)\n443\t\treturn vf\n444\t}\n445\t\n446\t// WithSuggestions sets custom suggestions for fixing the validation failure.\n447\t//\n448\t// This method allows you to provide domain-specific, actionable recommendations for\n449\t// resolving the validation error. Custom suggestions override the auto-generated\n450\t// suggestions that would normally be created based on the validation type and values.\n451\t//\n452\t// # Parameters\n453\t//\n454\t//   - suggestions: Variable number of suggestion strings\n455\t//\n456\t//     Best practices for suggestions:\n457\t//     - Be specific and actionable (not just \"check the value\")\n458\t//     - Provide concrete steps to fix the issue\n459\t//     - Include debugging tips when relevant\n460\t//     - Reference documentation or resources when applicable\n461\t//     - Keep suggestions concise but informative\n462\t//     - Order from most to least likely solution\n463\t//\n464\t// # When to Use Custom Suggestions\n465\t//\n466\t// Use this method when:\n467\t//   - Domain-specific knowledge is needed (not generic suggestions)\n468\t//   - You want to guide users to specific solutions\n469\t//   - Auto-generated suggestions would be too generic\n470\t//   - You need to reference internal documentation or systems\n471\t//   - The fix requires specific domain expertise\n472\t//\n473\t// # Returns\n474\t//\n475\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n476\t//\n477\t// # Example\n478\t//\n479\t//\t// Domain-specific suggestions\n480\t//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n481\t//\t    \"Check if the OAuth token has expired\",\n482\t//\t    \"Verify the token has 'read:users' scope\",\n483\t//\t    \"Contact admin to refresh permissions\",\n484\t//\t)\n485\t//\n486\t//\t// Specific debugging steps\n487\t//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n488\t//\t    \"Check service logs at /var/log/api/errors.log\",\n489\t//\t    \"Verify database connection in config.yaml\",\n490\t//\t    \"Test with curl: curl -v http://localhost:8080/health\",\n491\t//\t)\n492\t//\n493\t//\t// Reference documentation\n494\t//\tformatter.WithSuggestions(\n495\t//\t    \"See API docs: https://docs.example.com/errors#404\",\n496\t//\t    \"Check troubleshooting guide: /docs/troubleshooting.md\",\n497\t//\t)\n498\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n499\t\tvf.customSuggestions = append(vf.customSuggestions, suggestions...)\n500\t\treturn vf\n501\t}\n502\t\n503\t// WithCategory sets the category for the validation error.\n504\t//\n505\t// This method specifies the high-level categorization of the validation error.\n506\t// If not set, the category will be auto-derived from the validation type.\n507\t//\n508\t// # Parameters\n509\t//\n510\t//   - category: The ErrorCategory value (e.g., CategoryHTTP, CategoryContent, etc.)\n511\t//\n512\t// # Returns\n513\t//\n514\t// The same ValidationFormatter instance for method chaining.\n515\t//\n516\t// # Example\n517\t//\n518\t//\tformatter.WithCategory(validate.CategoryHTTP)\n519\t//\n520\t//\t// With chaining\n521\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n522\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n523\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n524\t//\t    WithCategory(validate.CategoryHTTP).\n525\t//\t    Format()\n526\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithCategory(category ErrorCategory) *ValidationFormatter {\n527\t\tvf.category = category\n528\t\treturn vf\n529\t}\n530\t\n531\t// Format creates the final ValidationError with all configured options.\n532\t//\n533\t// This is the final method in the builder chain that constructs the ValidationError\n534\t// with all the options that have been set. The returned ValidationError implements\n535\t// the error interface and can be returned directly or used in error handling.\n536\t//\n537\t// # Behavior\n538\t//\n539\t// The Format() method:\n540\t//   - Uses custom suggestions if provided via WithSuggestions\n541\t//   - Auto-generates suggestions if no custom ones were set\n542\t//   - Includes all fields that were set via With* methods\n543\t//   - Returns a ValidationError ready for use\n544\t//   - Implements error interface for immediate use\n545\t//\n546\t// # Auto-generated Suggestions\n547\t//\n548\t// If WithSuggestions was not called, suggestions are auto-generated based on:\n549\t//   - validationType (e.g., status_code gets endpoint-checking suggestions)\n550\t//   - expected and actual values (e.g., 404 gets resource-not-found suggestions)\n551\t//   - Common patterns for the validation category\n552\t//\n553\t// # Returns\n554\t//\n555\t// A ValidationError struct populated with all configured fields that implements\n556\t// the error interface and can be used immediately in error handling, logging, or\n557\t// test assertions.\n558\t//\n559\t// # Example\n560\t//\n561\t//\t// Complete usage example\n562\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n563\t//\t    WithExpected(200).\n564\t//\t    WithActual(404).\n565\t//\t    WithContext(\"GET /api/users/123\").\n566\t//\t    Format()\n567\t//\n568\t//\t// Error() method will produce:\n569\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n570\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n571\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n572\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n573\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n574\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n575\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n576\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n577\t//\n578\t//\t// Can be used directly as an error\n579\t//\treturn err\n580\t//\n581\t//\t// Or formatted for logging\n582\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Validation failed: %v\", err)\n583\t// formatErrorCore is the core error formatting function with enum-based category lookup.\n584\t// This function implements the central category lookup logic using GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum\n585\t// to map error types to their categories. It handles unknown error types by returning CategoryCustom\n586\t// as the default category.\n587\t//\n588\t// This is a low-level function that provides type-safe error type to category mapping.\n589\t// Most code should use ValidationFormatter.Format() or the Format*Error() convenience functions\n590\t// instead of calling this directly.\n591\t//\n592\t// Parameters:\n593\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n594\t//   - message: The error message\n595\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n596\t//   - expected: Optional expected value\n597\t//   - actual: Optional actual value\n598\t//   - context: Optional context information\n599\t//\n600\t// Returns a ValidationError with the category automatically derived from the error type.\n601\t//\n602\t// Example usage:\n603\t//\n604\t//\terr := formatErrorCore(\n605\t//\t    ErrTypeRequired,\n606\t//\t    \"Field is required\",\n607\t//\t    \"email\",\n608\t//\t    nil,\n609\t//\t    \"\",\n610\t//\t    \"user creation\",\n611\t//\t)\n612\t//\t// Returns ValidationError with Category = CategoryValidation\n613\tfunc formatErrorCore(\n614\t\terrorType ErrorType,\n615\t\tmessage string,\n616\t\tfieldName string,\n617\t\texpected interface{},\n618\t\tactual interface{},\n619\t\tcontext string,\n620\t) ValidationError {\n621\t\t// Lookup category using the error type enum\n622\t\t// GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum handles unknown error types by returning CategoryCustom\n623\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n624\t\n625\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for ValidationError\n626\t\terrorTypeStr := string(errorType)\n627\t\n628\t\t// Create and return ValidationError with populated category\n629\t\treturn ValidationError{\n630\t\t\tErrorType: errorTypeStr,\n631\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n632\t\t\tCategory:  category,\n633\t\t\tExpected:  expected,\n634\t\t\tActual:    actual,\n635\t\t\tContext:   context,\n636\t\t\tFieldName: fieldName,\n637\t\t}\n638\t}\n639\t\n640\tfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) Format() ValidationError {\n641\t\tsuggestions := vf.customSuggestions\n642\t\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n643\t\t\t// Use auto-generated suggestions if no custom ones provided\n644\t\t\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n645\t\t}\n646\t\n647\t\t// Generate a concise message from validation parts\n648\t\tmessage := generateMessageFromParts(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n649\t\n650\t\t// Determine category - use explicitly set category or auto-derive from error type\n651\t\tcategory := vf.category\n652\t\tif category == \"\" {\n653\t\t\tcategory = GetCategoryForErrorType(vf.validationType)\n654\t\t}\n655\t\n656\t\t// Construct ValidationError directly to support custom suggestions\n657\t\treturn ValidationError{\n658\t\t\tErrorType:         vf.validationType,\n659\t\t\tMessage:           message,\n660\t\t\tCategory:          category,\n661\t\t\tExpected:          vf.expected,\n662\t\t\tActual:            vf.actual,\n663\t\t\tContext:           vf.context,\n664\t\t\tResponseSnippet:   vf.responseSnippet,\n665\t\t\tFieldName:         vf.fieldName,\n666\t\t\tPatternDetails:    vf.patternDetails,\n667\t\t\tRangeInfo:         vf.rangeInfo,\n668\t\t\tValidationDetails: vf.validationDetails,\n669\t\t\tSuggestions:       suggestions,\n670\t\t}\n671\t}\n672\t\n673\t// =============================================================================\n674\t// EXTENDING THE FORMATTER FOR NEW VALIDATION TYPES\n675\t// =============================================================================\n676\t//\n677\t// This section explains how to extend the ValidationFormatter to support new\n678\t// validation types beyond the built-in convenience functions.\n679\t//\n680\t// # Creating Custom Validation Types\n681\t//\n682\t// To add support for a new validation type, you have several options:\n683\t//\n684\t// ## Option 1: Use the Builder Pattern Directly\n685\t//\n686\t// The simplest approach is to use NewValidationFormatter with your custom type:\n687\t//\n688\t//\t// Custom header validation\n689\t//\terr := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_header\").\n690\t//\t    WithExpected(\"X-Custom-Header: required\").\n691\t//\t    WithActual(\"missing\").\n692\t//\t    WithContext(\"API request validation\").\n693\t//\t    WithFieldName(\"X-Custom-Header\").\n694\t//\t    WithSuggestions(\"Add X-Custom-Header to requests\", \"See API docs\").\n695\t//\t    Format()\n696\t//\n697\t// ## Option 2: Create a Convenience Function\n698\t//\n699\t// For commonly used validation types, create a convenience function following\n700\t// the pattern of existing functions like FormatStatusCodeError:\n701\t//\n702\t//\t// FormatCustomHeaderError validates custom HTTP headers\n703\t//\tfunc FormatCustomHeaderError(headerName, expected, actual, context string) validate.ValidationError {\n704\t//\t    return validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"custom_header\").\n705\t//\t        WithExpected(expected).\n706\t//\t        WithActual(actual).\n707\t//\t        WithFieldName(headerName).\n708\t//\t        WithContext(context).\n709\t//\t        WithSuggestions(\n710\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Add %s header to request\", headerName),\n711\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Expected value: %s\", expected),\n712\t//\t        ).\n713\t//\t        Format()\n714\t//\t}\n715\t//\n716\t// ## Option 3: Use FormatCustomValidationError\n717\t//\n718\t// For advanced scenarios requiring full control, use FormatCustomValidationError:\n719\t//\n720\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n721\t//\t    \"response_time\",\n722\t//\t    \"< 100ms\",\n723\t//\t    \"250ms\",\n724\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"API performance check\"),\n725\t//\t    validate.WithValidationDetails(\"SLA requires < 100ms response time\"),\n726\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Optimize database queries\", \"Add caching\"),\n727\t//\t)\n728\t//\n729\t// # Best Practices for New Validation Types\n730\t//\n731\t// When creating custom validation types:\n732\t//\n733\t// 1. **Use descriptive type names**: Choose clear, descriptive validation type names\n734\t//    that indicate what was validated (e.g., \"response_time\", \"header_presence\").\n735\t//\n736\t// 2. **Provide specific suggestions**: Custom suggestions should be actionable and\n737\t//    specific to your domain rather than generic.\n738\t//\n739\t// 3. **Include relevant context**: Always provide context about where/when the\n740\t//    validation occurred to aid debugging.\n741\t//\n742\t// 4. **Use appropriate field types**: Match the expected/actual field types to what\n743\t//    you're validating (int for codes, string for patterns, etc.).\n744\t//\n745\t// 5. **Consider validation details**: Use WithValidationDetails for complex\n746\t//    validations with multiple steps or conditions.\n747\t//\n748\t// 6. **Document your validation type**: If creating a convenience function, document\n749\t//    it thoroughly like the existing convenience functions.\n750\t//\n751\t// # Integration with Auto-generated Suggestions\n752\t//\n753\t// The ValidationFormatter automatically generates suggestions based on validation\n754\t// type when you don't provide custom suggestions. To integrate your custom type\n755\t// with this system, you can modify the generateSuggestions function to recognize\n756\t// your new type and provide domain-appropriate suggestions.\n757\t//\n758\t// Current auto-generated suggestion patterns:\n759\t//   - \"status_code\": Endpoint and resource verification suggestions\n760\t//   - \"error_message\": Error analysis and debugging suggestions\n761\t//   - \"content_type\": Header and format verification suggestions\n762\t//   - \"status_code_range\": Range-specific debugging suggestions\n763\t//\n764\t// # Example: Adding a New Validation Type\n765\t//\n766\t// Here's a complete example of adding a \"response_time\" validation type:\n767\t//\n768\t//\t// 1. Create the convenience function\n769\t//\tfunc FormatResponseTimeError(expectedMaxMs, actualMs int, endpoint string) validate.ValidationError {\n770\t//\t    return validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"response_time\").\n771\t//\t        WithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"< %dms\", expectedMaxMs)).\n772\t//\t        WithActual(fmt.Sprintf(\"%dms\", actualMs)).\n773\t//\t        WithContext(endpoint).\n774\t//\t        WithValidationDetails(\n775\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"SLA requirement: < %dms\", expectedMaxMs),\n776\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"Actual response time: %dms\", actualMs),\n777\t//\t            fmt.Sprintf(\"SLA violation: %dms over limit\", actualMs-expectedMaxMs),\n778\t//\t        ).\n779\t//\t        WithSuggestions(\n780\t//\t            \"Check for slow database queries\",\n781\t//\t            \"Verify network latency\",\n782\t//\t            \"Review application performance metrics\",\n783\t//\t            \"Consider implementing caching\",\n784\t//\t        ).\n785\t//\t        Format()\n786\t//\t}\n787\t//\n788\t//\t// 2. Use it in your code\n789\t//\terr := FormatResponseTimeError(100, 250, \"GET /api/users\")\n790\t//\tlog.Printf(\"Performance validation failed: %v\", err)\n791\t//\n792\t// This approach ensures your custom validation types integrate seamlessly with\n793\t// the existing error formatting system while providing domain-specific guidance.\n794\t\n795\t// =============================================================================\n796\t// CONVENIENCE FUNCTIONS FOR COMMON VALIDATION SCENARIOS\n797\t// =============================================================================\n798\t\n799\t// FormatStatusCodeError creates a validation error for HTTP status code mismatches.\n800\t//\n801\t// Parameters:\n802\t//   - expected: The expected status code (e.g., 200) or codes (e.g., []int{200, 201})\n803\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n804\t//   - context: Optional context about the request (e.g., \"GET /api/users\")\n805\t//\n806\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n807\t//\n808\t// Example usage:\n809\t//\n810\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeError(200, 404, \"GET /api/users/123\")\n811\t//\t// Output:\n812\t//\t// status_code validation failed\n813\t//\t//   Expected: 200 (OK)\n814\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n815\t//\t//   Context:  GET /api/users/123\n816\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n817\t//\t//     - Verify the endpoint URL is correct\n818\t//\t//     - Check if the resource ID or identifier exists\n819\t//\t//     - Ensure the resource hasn't been deleted or moved\n820\tfunc FormatStatusCodeError(expected interface{}, actual int, context string) ValidationError {\n821\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n822\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n823\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n824\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n825\t\t\tFormat()\n826\t}\n827\t\n828\t// FormatErrorMessageError creates a validation error for error message pattern mismatches.\n829\t//\n830\t// Parameters:\n831\t//   - expectedPattern: The pattern that was expected (e.g., \"invalid.*token\", \"not found\")\n832\t//   - actualMessage: The actual error message received\n833\t//   - fieldName: The field where the error was found (e.g., \"error\", \"message\")\n834\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n835\t//\n836\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n837\t//\n838\t// Example usage:\n839\t//\n840\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorMessageError(\"invalid.*token\", \"access_denied\", \"error\", \"OAuth validation\")\n841\t//\t// Output:\n842\t//\t// error_message validation failed\n843\t//\t//   Expected: invalid.*token\n844\t//\t//   Actual:   access_denied\n845\t//\t//   Context:  OAuth validation\n846\t//\t//   Field:    error\n847\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n848\t//\t//     - Review the error message for specific details\n849\t//\t//     - Check API documentation for this error type\n850\t//\t//     - Verify request parameters match requirements\n851\tfunc FormatErrorMessageError(expectedPattern, actualMessage, fieldName, context string) ValidationError {\n852\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"error_message\").\n853\t\t\tWithExpected(expectedPattern).\n854\t\t\tWithActual(actualMessage).\n855\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n856\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n857\t\t\tFormat()\n858\t}\n859\t\n860\t// FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n861\t//\n862\t// Parameters:\n863\t//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n864\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n865\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n866\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name being validated (e.g., \"status_code\", \"response_code\")\n867\t//\n868\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and suggestions.\n869\t//\n870\t// Example usage:\n871\t//\n872\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeRangeError(\"4xx\", 200, \"error response check\", \"status_code\")\n873\t//\t// Output:\n874\t//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n875\t//\t//   Field:    status_code\n876\t//\t//   Expected: 4xx (400-499)\n877\t//\t//   Actual:   200\n878\t//\t//   Context:  error response check\n879\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n880\t//\t//     - Review request parameters for errors\n881\t//\t//     - Check authentication credentials\n882\t//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n883\tfunc FormatStatusCodeRangeError(pattern string, actual int, context string, fieldName string) ValidationError {\n884\t\tmin, max, desc, err := getRangeInfo(pattern)\n885\t\n886\t\t// Build range info string\n887\t\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", min, max)\n888\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n889\t\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", min, max, desc)\n890\t\t}\n891\t\n892\t\tdetails := []string{}\n893\t\tif err != nil {\n894\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Pattern error: %v\", err))\n895\t\t} else {\n896\t\t\tdetails = append(details, fmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside range %d-%d\", actual, min, max))\n897\t\t}\n898\t\n899\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n900\t\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", pattern, desc)).\n901\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n902\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n903\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n904\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...)\n905\t\n906\t\t// Only add range info if the pattern is valid\n907\t\tif err == nil {\n908\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(rangeInfo)\n909\t\t}\n910\t\n911\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n912\t}\n913\t\n914\t// FormatContentTypeError creates a validation error for Content-Type header mismatches.\n915\t//\n916\t// Parameters:\n917\t//   - expected: The expected content type (e.g., \"application/json\")\n918\t//   - actual: The actual content type received\n919\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n920\t//\n921\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n922\t//\n923\t// Example usage:\n924\t//\n925\t//\terr := validate.FormatContentTypeError(\"application/json\", \"text/html\", \"API response\")\n926\t//\t// Output:\n927\t//\t// content_type validation failed\n928\t//\t//   Expected: application/json\n929\t//\t//   Actual:   text/html\n930\t//   Context:  API response\n931\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n932\t//\t//     - Verify Content-Type header matches request body format\n933\t//\t//     - Check if charset or boundary parameters are needed\n934\t//\t//     - Ensure the body is properly formatted for the content type\n935\tfunc FormatContentTypeError(expected, actual, context string) ValidationError {\n936\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n937\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n938\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n939\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n940\t\t\tFormat()\n941\t}\n942\t\n943\t// FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError creates a validation error for min/max range validation failures.\n944\t//\n945\t// This function is used when validating status codes (or other numeric values) against\n946\t// explicit minimum and maximum boundaries. It provides enhanced error messages showing\n947\t// the exact range that was expected and the actual value that was received.\n948\t//\n949\t// Parameters:\n950\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value (inclusive)\n951\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value (inclusive)\n952\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that was received\n953\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation (e.g., \"status code validation\")\n954\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name being validated (e.g., \"status_code\", \"response_code\")\n955\t//\n956\t// Returns a ValidationError with range information and appropriate suggestions.\n957\t//\n958\t// Example usage:\n959\t//\n960\t//\terr := validate.FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError(200, 299, 404, \"success response check\", \"status_code\")\n961\t//\t// Output:\n962\t//\t// status_code_range validation failed\n963\t//\t//   Field:    status_code\n964\t//\t//   Expected: 200-299 (Success)\n965\t//\t//   Actual:   404 (Not Found)\n966\t//\t//   Range:    200-299 (Success)\n967\t//\t//   Context:  success response check\n968\t//\t//   Validation Details:\n969\t//\t//     - Status code 404 is outside valid range 200-299\n970\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n971\t//\t//     - Check if the endpoint URL is correct\n972\t//\t//     - Verify the resource exists and is accessible\n973\t//\t//     - Ensure authentication credentials are valid\n974\tfunc FormatStatusCodeMinRangeError(minValue, maxValue, actualValue int, context string, fieldName string) ValidationError {\n975\t\t// Get range description\n976\t\tdesc := getRangeDescriptionForValues(minValue, maxValue)\n977\t\n978\t\t// Build range info string\n979\t\trangeInfo := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minValue, maxValue)\n980\t\tif desc != \"\" {\n981\t\t\trangeInfo = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d (%s)\", minValue, maxValue, desc)\n982\t\t}\n983\t\n984\t\tdetails := []string{\n985\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Status code %d is outside valid range %d-%d\", actualValue, minValue, maxValue),\n986\t\t}\n987\t\n988\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n989\t\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minValue, maxValue)).\n990\t\t\tWithActual(actualValue).\n991\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n992\t\t\tWithFieldName(fieldName).\n993\t\t\tWithRangeInfo(rangeInfo).\n994\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...).\n995\t\t\tFormat()\n996\t}\n997\t\n998\t// getRangeDescriptionForValues returns a human-readable description for a status code range.\n999\t// This helper provides category descriptions for common status code ranges.\n1000\tfunc getRangeDescriptionForValues(minCode, maxCode int) string {\n1001\t\tranges := map[string]string{\n1002\t\t\t\"200-299\": \"Success\",\n1003\t\t\t\"300-399\": \"Redirection\",\n1004\t\t\t\"400-499\": \"Client Error\",\n1005\t\t\t\"500-599\": \"Server Error\",\n1006\t\t\t\"100-199\": \"Informational\",\n1007\t\t}\n1008\t\n1009\t\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d-%d\", minCode, maxCode)\n1010\t\tif desc, ok := ranges[key]; ok {\n1011\t\t\treturn desc\n1012\t\t}\n1013\t\n1014\t\treturn \"\"\n1015\t\n1016\t}\n1017\t// FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\n1018\t//\n1019\t// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\n1020\t// custom suggestions. Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\n1021\t// your specific requirements.\n1022\t//\n1023\t// Parameters:\n1024\t//   - validationType: The category of validation\n1025\t//   - expected: The expected value\n1026\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n1027\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions to customize the error\n1028\t//\n1029\t// Example usage:\n1030\t//\n1031\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n1032\t//\t    \"custom_field\",\n1033\t//\t    \"required_value\",\n1034\t//\t    \"actual_value\",\n1035\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n1036\t//\t    validate.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"field\": \"actual_value\"}`),\n1037\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\", \"Verify configuration\"),\n1038\t//\t)\n1039\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n1040\t\tvalidationType string,\n1041\t\texpected, actual interface{},\n1042\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n1043\t) ValidationError {\n1044\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n1045\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n1046\t\t\topt(config)\n1047\t\t}\n1048\t\n1049\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n1050\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n1051\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n1052\t\n1053\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n1054\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n1055\t\t}\n1056\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n1057\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n1058\t\t}\n1059\t\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n1060\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n1061\t\t}\n1062\t\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n1063\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n1064\t\t}\n1065\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n1066\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n1067\t\t}\n1068\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n1069\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n1070\t\t}\n1071\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n1072\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n1073\t\t}\n1074\t\n1075\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n1076\t}\n1077\t\n1078\t// =============================================================================\n1079\t// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\n1080\t// =============================================================================\n1081\t\n1082\t// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\n1083\ttype FormatConfig struct {\n1084\t\tContext            string\n1085\t\tResponseSnippet    string\n1086\t\tFieldName          string\n1087\t\tPatternDetails     string\n1088\t\tRangeInfo          string\n1089\t\tValidationDetails  []string\n1090\t\tSuggestions        []string\n1091\t\t// Category-aware and severity support\n1092\t\tSeverityOverride   ErrorSeverity\n1093\t\tCategoryHint       ErrorCategory\n1094\t\tStatusCode         int\n1095\t\tTimeout            int\n1096\t\tSecurityContext    string\n1097\t}\n1098\t\n1099\t// FormatOption is a function that configures a FormatConfig.\n1100\ttype FormatOption func(*FormatConfig)\n1101\t\n1102\t// WithContext creates a FormatOption that sets the validation context.\n1103\tfunc WithContext(context string) FormatOption {\n1104\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1105\t\t\tc.Context = context\n1106\t\t}\n1107\t}\n1108\t\n1109\t// WithResponseSnippet creates a FormatOption that sets the response snippet.\n1110\tfunc WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) FormatOption {\n1111\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1112\t\t\tc.ResponseSnippet = snippet\n1113\t\t}\n1114\t}\n1115\t\n1116\t// WithFieldName creates a FormatOption that sets the field name.\n1117\tfunc WithFieldName(fieldName string) FormatOption {\n1118\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1119\t\t\tc.FieldName = fieldName\n1120\t\t}\n1121\t}\n1122\t\n1123\t// WithPatternDetails creates a FormatOption that sets pattern details.\n1124\tfunc WithPatternDetails(details string) FormatOption {\n1125\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1126\t\t\tc.PatternDetails = details\n1127\t\t}\n1128\t}\n1129\t\n1130\t// WithRangeInfo creates a FormatOption that sets range information.\n1131\tfunc WithRangeInfo(info string) FormatOption {\n1132\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1133\t\t\tc.RangeInfo = info\n1134\t\t}\n1135\t}\n1136\t\n1137\t// WithValidationDetails creates a FormatOption that adds validation details.\n1138\tfunc WithValidationDetails(details ...string) FormatOption {\n1139\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1140\t\t\tc.ValidationDetails = append(c.ValidationDetails, details...)\n1141\t\t}\n1142\t}\n1143\t\n1144\t// WithSuggestions creates a FormatOption that sets custom suggestions.\n1145\tfunc WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) FormatOption {\n1146\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1147\t\t\tc.Suggestions = append(c.Suggestions, suggestions...)\n1148\t\t}\n1149\t}\n1150\t\n1151\t// WithSeverityOverride creates a FormatOption that overrides the default severity level.\n1152\t// Use this when you want to specify a custom severity instead of using the error type's default.\n1153\t//\n1154\t// Example usage:\n1155\t//\n1156\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1157\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n1158\t//\t    \"Field is required\",\n1159\t//\t    \"email\",\n1160\t//\t    validate.WithSeverityOverride(validate.SeverityCritical),\n1161\t//\t)\n1162\tfunc WithSeverityOverride(severity ErrorSeverity) FormatOption {\n1163\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1164\t\t\tc.SeverityOverride = severity\n1165\t\t}\n1166\t}\n1167\t\n1168\t// WithCategoryHint creates a FormatOption that provides a category hint for formatting.\n1169\t// Use this when you want to specify a custom category instead of using the error type's default.\n1170\t//\n1171\t// Example usage:\n1172\t//\n1173\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1174\t//\t WithCategoryHint(category ErrorCategory) FormatOption {\n1180\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1181\t\t\tc.CategoryHint = category\n1182\t\t}\n1183\t}\n1184\t\n1185\t// WithStatusCode creates a FormatOption that sets the HTTP status code for HTTP errors.\n1186\t// Use this with HTTP-related errors to include status code information in the formatted message.\n1187\t//\n1188\t// Example usage:\n1189\t//\n1190\t//\terr := validate.FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(\n1191\t//\t    validate.ErrTypeRequired,\n1192\t//\t    \"Resource not found\",\n1193\t//\t    \"endpoint\",\n1194\t//\t    validate.WithStatusCode(404),\n1195\t//\t)\n1196\tfunc WithStatusCode(statusCode int) FormatOption {\n1197\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n1198\t\t\tc.StatusCode = statusCode\n1199\t\t}\n1200\t}\n1201\t\n1202\t// WithTimeout creates a FormatOption that sets the timeout for performance errors.\n1203\t// Use this with performance-related errors to include timeout
